nodes.context: Umbrella term for Romanian organized crime groups active in Europe and the Americas, involved in human trafficking, drug trafficking, and financial crimes.
Romanian criminal group in Turin, Italy, specialized in extortion, robbery, and drug trafficking.
Mexican drug cartel; has a lucrative pact with Romanian mafia gangs in the Americas.
Italian mafia organization; collaborates with Romanian mafia in drug trafficking in Europe.
Headed by Ion Balint (Nuțu Cămătaru) and his brother Vasile Balint (Sile Cămătaru), involved in usury, prostitution, blackmail in Sector 5, Bucharest.
Led by Aurel Răducan, active in Pantelimon (Sector 2) and Cluj-Napoca, involved in usury and prostitution.
Headed by Radu Roșca, dominant in Sector 4, involved in blackmail, usury, protection rackets, security services, real estate.
Led by brothers Licuță and Dănuț Udilă, operates in Sectors 1 and 4, involved in real estate and cigarette smuggling.
Led by Aurel Chira, controls Sector 3, involved in usury, drug trafficking, prostitution.
One of the largest in Sectors 2 and 6, involved in prostitution, usury, blackmail; formerly led by Ion Duduianu (Segher).
Led by Ion Voinea (Ion al lui Ștoacă), active in Sectors 4 and 5, specializes in international drug trafficking and prostitution.
Led by Gheorghe Dincă and his son Vasile Dincă (Miky), based in Sector 6, involved in protection rackets, debt collection, real estate.
Led by Vasile Domnu (Liderică), controlled prostitution in Bucharest's Gara de Nord area; involved in feud with Sadoveanu clan.
Mentioned as rival of Domnu clan in a feud.

edges.context: Romanian mafia gangs in the Americas have built a lucrative pact with the Sinaloa Cartel.
Romanian mafia collaborates with 'Ndrangheta to distribute narcotics throughout Europe.
Vancică clan formed an alliance with Sportivilor clan to challenge the Cămătari clan.
Vancică clan expanded into Cămătari territories after their imprisonment, leading to rivalry.
Domnu clan was involved in a violent feud with Sadoveanu clan.
